title: baccharis incarum
intro: Baccharis incarum, also known as Ecuadorian dwarf baccharis, is a small shrub-like plant that belongs to the daisy family and is native to the Andean region of South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Baccharis incarum include its ability to reduce fever, relieve respiratory issues, and support digestive health. Baccharis incarum is often prepared as a tea or infusion by steeping the leaves and stems in hot water, and it can also be found in herbal supplement form.
